TEXT.source: NTU Corpus stories for Kanakanavu, story: kkvNr_fishing_Pani

S.FORM: ’aa sua nganai maku ia Pani.
S.TRANSL: 嗯，我的名字是Pani。
Well, my name is Pani.

S.FORM: ’aa mia tati’iing pa ku kipapa kia mamarang.
S.TRANSL: 我小時候跟隨著長輩們。
I followed the seniors when I was little.

S.FORM: uturu iikita tamna Kanakanavu siarupaca mara vutuukuru.
S.TRANSL: 我們kanakanavu捕魚蝦的工具有三種。
We Kanakanavu have three ways of catching fish and shrimps.

S.FORM: ucani ia pinaarupu pinarupuun sua vutuukuru.
S.TRANSL: 第一個方式是垂釣，魚用吊的。
One is angling, which hangs fish up.

S.FORM: ucani ia pusu’uriing.
S.TRANSL: 第二種方式是用火把照明。
Another is pusu’uriing.

S.FORM: sua pusu’uriing ia ’uruupaca noo mapitʉ’ʉ cu taniara mavici ’uringi mookusa na cakʉran.
S.TRANSL: 所謂用火把照明是利用天暗之後，帶著芒草莖去河裡(溪裡)。
The so-called pusu’uriing is that one brings the stalks of Japanese silvergrass to rivers partucularly after it gets dark in the evening.

S.FORM: ’uru ’uruupaca nguain tamna sinaang cumacʉ’ʉra na nungnung nukai cakʉran tamna canuum.
S.TRANSL: 利用他的亮光來看河流或者溪流的水。
And (He) uses his light to beam at the water of rivers.

S.FORM: noo ’esiin nia vutuukuru nukai ’arasakai ia mara cu kia caraa’acʉ sapuarʉ mara vutuukuru.
S.TRANSL: 那裏若有魚蝦，我們就拿著魚叉或撈網來補魚。
If there are fish and shrimps, we will takes fish spears and fishing nets to catch fish.

S.FORM: makai um maseen ia neen kusai vanai Kanakanavu maraa ’aan cu pusukiriing.
S.TRANSL: 但是，究竟什麼原因，現在的kanakanavu人不再用火把照明來(捕魚)了?
But, why on earth do Kanakanavu nowadays no longer pusukiriing?

S.FORM: sua ucani ia tumatuuncu.
S.TRANSL: 再來一種方式是毒魚。
The other is tumatuuncu.

S.FORM: ka’aan tia upeen takananga vutuukuru mataa ’arasakai ia ka’aan mamacai nakai arapipining na canumu makai naturu cu.
S.TRANSL: 不用多久，魚跟蝦子不會死，但是會浮出水面上，很像呆癡了。
Before long, fish and shrimps won’t die but will float from the water, becoming dull.

S.FORM: meesua ia cau ia ’uruupaca kangvang caraa’acʉ mataa sapuarʉ umara vutuukuru.
S.TRANSL: 在那個時候，人們就用魚叉或撈網來捕魚。
At that time, people use fish spears amd fishing nets to catch fish.

S.FORM: er manasʉ cenana ia makaan nukai makacusa maa cau tumatuncu miana arakukun kangvang mookusa ’inia.
S.TRANSL: 嗯，以前或許一年一次或者兩次大家一起前往去毒魚。
Well, in the past, all poeple t<um>a-tuncu together maybe once or twice a year.

S.FORM: sua mastaan ka’anmana maku matipa ia pinaarupu.
S.TRANSL: 我最喜歡參加的是釣魚。
What I like to join most is fishing.

S.FORM: noo makasiin mamarang tuu Pani nura ia te kita pinaarupu misee iak! aranai meesua ku te ci kia sii macangcangarʉ ia ah mariitarʉ miseen ka’aan pa aramia’ʉ te cu kia mook mookusa ’inia.
S.TRANSL: 如果有長輩說，Pani我們明天要釣魚，啊，我就在那裏非常開心，一直等待著怎麼還沒起床，因為想要去了。
If there is a senior says “Pani, we will go fishing tomorrow,” ah, I will be very happy at that time and keep waiting, thinking why we still don’t get up because I’ve wanted to go.

S.FORM: nakai noo pinarupiin ia te kita mati’araang peecapa narupu.
S.TRANSL: 但是如果要釣魚，我們要準備吊桿、魚鉤。
But if we want to fish, we need to prepare a fishing pole and hook.

S.FORM: sua peecapa Kanakanavu miana ia ’uruupaca kim ta’uici ta’uici tia mata’ʉ umara tikiriim kia ʉncoo maraa ma’inacʉ to’umaang arʉʉn te pa cunuun ti’iing.
S.TRANSL: 以前Kanakanavu的釣竿是使用細的桂竹，要生鮮的，我們尋找比較直的竹子，拿了之後還要稍微用火烤。
Kanavuravu is a kind of sinocalamus latiflorus. In fact, the one who goes fishing will use kapani.

S.FORM: tia macici ta’iing noo macici cu ia tia suma’inacʉn makaasua ʉncoo capa ia mamanʉng.
S.TRANSL: 要熱一點，熱了以後，要把它變直。像那樣的釣竿才是好的。
The temperature should be a little higher. After it gets heated, one needs to straighten the bamboo. Such a fishing pole is good.

S.FORM: te pa kita mati’araang ʉ’ʉ neen sua kʉcʉ iihua? narupu narupu.
S.TRANSL: 我們還要準備，那是什麼啊，對啦，魚鉤。
We still need to prepare...what’s that...yup, the hook.

S.FORM: ’una cu capa racee ʉn ’una cu cepa narupu hau te kita mo cʉmcʉm aramia’ʉ.
S.TRANSL: 有了釣竿、魚鉤，我們要很早起床。
There are fishing poles and hooks. We have to get up early.

S.FORM: tia maica na ra ra ratakʉʉtakʉ sii ’inia ia ’una ngica’ʉ.
S.TRANSL: 要經過泥濘的地方，因為那裏有蚯蚓。
(We) need to pass through the muddy area because there are earthworms.

S.FORM: tia matisa’ʉ maan mataarava mapusan ngica’ʉ umaavici na cakʉran.
S.TRANSL: 要抓十到二十條的蚯蚓，帶到河裡。
(We) need to catch ten to twelve earthworms and bring them to the river.

S.FORM: noo mookusa cu cakʉran ia arʉʉn cu ngica’ʉ iihua ʉsʉ’ʉn na narupu matiikusa na canumu.
S.TRANSL: 到了河裡之後，就拿那個蚯蚓放在魚鉤上，拋向水裡。
After arriving in the river, we take that earthworm and put it on the hook and toss (the hook) toward the water.

S.FORM: tumatimana ku marang mamarang miana sua vutuukuru kaan ia sua maraa tatia ia monoon na ʉncoo mintasʉ canuum.
S.TRANSL: 我聽以前的老人家說比較大的魚經常待在...那個...比較湍急的水。
it is said that bigger fish often stay...well...in rapid currents.

S.FORM: ’apanapa ku moon nia moon kangvang na mintasʉ canuum pinaarupu nakai ma’icʉpʉ ku noo matikocu canuum narupu na vatu ia ka’aan cu tarʉ’ʉn arʉʉn moon cu kia nia muru’inasʉ.
S.TRANSL: 我也嘗試在湍急的河流釣魚，但是又擔心魚鉤鉤住石頭而拉不回來，我就會在那邊很困擾。
I sometimes fish in more rapid water. But I’m afraid that the hook gets stuck among the stones and cannot be pulled back. I will be bothered there.

S.FORM: imoo maraa manmaan ku moon na niatumura ra canumu moon nia pinaarupu.
S.TRANSL: 所以，我比較喜歡在深潭，待在那裡釣魚。
So, I like to fish in deep water more.

S.FORM: nakai na niatumura canumu ia cucuru maraa ka’aan tatia sua vutuukuru ’inia.
S.TRANSL: 但是在深潭那裏的魚的確比較不大。
but the fish in deep water there truely are relatively not big.

S.FORM: miana noo arakukun kim mamarurang pinaarupu noo mana’ʉ cu vutuukuru na capa mia ia manasʉ makasi cu kimia marang Pani umara maringuna karu nukai to’umaang mosa na cikiiringa isa matupuru.
S.TRANSL: 以前我們若跟著老一輩的人一起釣魚，當我們釣的魚已經到魚簍的一半的時候，通常我們的老人家就會呼喊著說：「Pani，你去拿乾材或者竹子到河流的邊邊上生火。」
In the past, when we went fishing with our seniors and the fish can fill half of the creel, the seniors would say "Pani, go take the dry wood and go there to make a fire."

S.FORM: ’aa! tavarʉ’ʉ cu ku.
S.TRANSL: 啊！我知道了。
“Ah, I get it.”

S.FORM: moon cu kim na cikiiringa nia mamanu ta ti’iing matuapuru.
S.TRANSL: 我們小朋友們就在河邊上升火。
We little kids then made a fire over there.

S.FORM: mova kimia mamarang sua vutuukuru.
S.TRANSL: 老人家就給我們魚。
The old people gave us the fish.

S.FORM: noo kana’amisan ia noo ’uneen canumu maraa mamanʉng kunaam ia mara pa kim ’uruupaca karu siuʉn ’inia umara ʉncoo kunaam.
S.TRANSL: 當冬天的時候，如果水中有好的川苔，我們就會使用樹枝把川苔捲起來。
When winter comes, if there is good moss in water, we will still pick the moss out with wood sticks.

S.FORM: po’ocipi kia ’aau noo musucikiiciki cu canumu ia ʉsʉ’ʉn nia kunaam.
S.TRANSL: 我們煮湯，當水沸騰了以後，我們就放入川苔。
We cook soup. When the water is boiling, we put the moss in.

S.FORM: ia’ah! mamarang tumatimana ku marang miana arapipining cu kari in makasi ’aa! kangvang na ’aau iisa isi mas taan sa’o’o misee.
S.TRANSL: 「啊!」以前我聽到老人家說出這樣的話:「啊!所有的湯裡面，這個湯是最好的。」
“Ah,” I heard the old people say like this in the past, “Ah, among all the kinds of soup, this (soup) is the best.”

S.FORM: ’aa! cucuru pacʉpʉcʉpʉngʉn miana siacʉvʉrʉ mita Kanakanavu kacangcangarʉn maku pacʉpʉcʉpʉngʉn.
S.TRANSL: 啊!想起來我們Kanakanavu以往的生活方式真的令我很快樂。
Ah, We think that we Kanakanavu’s way of living in the past is reminiscent whenever I recall.

S.FORM: imoo makai iisi ’aa! to’onaa mita te mita patimanʉngʉn sii mataarava nuari tia ’una kangvang manu mita ’una maningcau ’ʉnai to’ona in cumacʉvʉrʉ aratatia.
S.TRANSL: 所以，像這些我們所住的地方，我們要把它弄得好好的，使我們的後代將來也有美好的地方可以生活成長。
So, such like the places we live in, we have to keep them well so as to let our offspring have good places to grow up.

S.FORM: ’aa! makaasua tia kari maku isi nguaci.
S.TRANSL: 啊！我要說的就是這樣，僅此。
Ah, this is what I want to say. That’s it.
